content.problemStatement:
Tumut, the best programmer in his village Applidz, invented a problem and decided to share it with you:

You are given two integer sequences S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, \dots, S_NS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äč and T1,T2,‚Ä¶,TMT_1, T_2, \dots, T_MT1‚Äč,T2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,TM‚Äč and an integer xxx. You are allowed to perform the following operation any number of times:


choose an element of SSS and an element of TTT (let's denote them by SiS_iSi‚Äč and TjT_jTj‚Äč respectively)
decrease both SiS_iSi‚Äč and TjT_jTj‚Äč by xxx, i.e. replace SiS_iSi‚Äč by Si‚ąíxS_i-xSi‚Äč‚ąíx and TjT_jTj‚Äč by Tj‚ąíxT_j-xTj‚Äč‚ąíx
Let's denote the minimum and maximum value in the sequence SSS after performing the chosen operations (possibly none) by minSminSminS and maxSmaxSmaxS respectively. Similarly, let's denote the minimum and maximum value in TTT after performing the chosen operations by minTminTminT and maxTmaxTmaxT respectively. The goal is minimizing the expression (maxS+maxT)‚ąí(minS+minT)(maxS+maxT) - (minS+minT)(maxS+maxT)‚ąí(minS+minT). Compute the minimum value of this expression.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and xxx.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers S1,S2‚Ä¶SNS_1, S_2 \dots S_NS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč‚Ä¶SN‚Äč.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers T1,T2‚Ä¶TMT_1, T_2 \dots T_MT1‚Äč,T2‚Äč‚Ä¶TM‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum possible value of the expression (maxS+maxT)‚ąí(minS+minT)(maxS+maxT) - (minS+minT)(maxS+maxT)‚ąí(minS+minT).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
We can perform these two operations:

decrease S2S_2S2‚Äč and T1T_1T1‚Äč by xxx
decrease S2S_2S2‚Äč and T2T_2T2‚Äč by xxx

Afterwards, the sequence SSS will be [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and the sequence TTT will be [‚ąí1,0][-1, 0][‚ąí1,0]. The resulting value of the given expression is (2+0)‚ąí(1+(‚ąí1))=2(2+0)-(1+(-1)) = 2(2+0)‚ąí(1+(‚ąí1))=2. It is impossible to obtain a smaller value no matter how many operations are performed.
